title: Audit Record Retention Compliance vs Policy

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 AU-11 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 AU-11 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.10: Audit Record Retention Compliance vs Policy.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.10' running on /services/data/indexes, lookup nist_retention_policy.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| lookup nist_retention_policy.csv title OUTPUT required_min_days, system_category
| eval compliant=if(retention_days+0>=required_min_days+0,"Yes","No")
| where compliant="No" OR isnull(required_min_days)
description: Compares configured frozen retention to policy targets for systems of record logs supporting AU-11 audit record retention.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Build `nist_retention_policy.csv` with index or prefix to required days; (2) reconcile with offline archive retention; (3) alert on non-compliance; (4) export for POA&M tracking.
visualization: Table (index, retention, required), Bar chart (gap days), Single value (non-compliant count)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `/services/data/indexes`, lookup `nist_retention_policy.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Build `nist_retention_policy.csv` with index or prefix to required days; (2) reconcile with offline archive retention; (3) alert on non-compliance; (4) export for POA&M tracking.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| lookup nist_retention_policy.csv title OUTPUT required_min_days, system_category
| eval compliant=if(retention_days+0>=required_min_days+0,"Yes","No")
| where compliant="No" OR isnull(required_min_days)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Audit Record Retention Compliance vs Policy (AU-11)** — Compares configured frozen retention to policy targets for systems of record logs supporting AU-11 audit record retention.

Documented **Data sources**: `/services/data/indexes`, lookup `nist_retention_policy.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **retention_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **compliant** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where compliant="No" OR isnull(required_min_days)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (index, retention, required), Bar chart (gap days), Single value (non-compliant count)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for compares configured frozen retention to policy targets for systems of record logs supporting audit record retention. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
